hypothesis: Fine-tuning the transformer model on a small set of experimentally measured ATR-IR mixture spectra augmented with concentration-dependent non-linear interaction terms—derived from systematically characterized hydrogen-bonding and dipole-dipole interacting compound pairs—will yield greater cross-domain accuracy improvements than fine-tuning on an equivalently sized set of randomly selected ATR-IR mixture spectra, because the spectral alignment network's reconstruction loss will more effectively learn to suppress intermolecular interaction artifacts when trained on examples where non-linear deviations from the linear combination approximation are explicitly represented and labeled by interaction type.
